title: Asia remains key target of energy shock impact – JP Morgan AM - Funds Global Asia
content: A month on from the start of the war between the US and Israel on one side and Iran on the other, Tai Hui, chief market strategist, Asia Pacific at JP Morgan Asset Management has highlighted that the ongoing energy crisis is hitting Asia hardest given the physical disruption that distinguishes the current challenge to that seen in 2022 sparked by Russia’s invasion of Ukraine. Then, it was Europe that “bore the brunt” as it relied on significant imports of oil an gas from Russia via pipelines, and sanctions subsequently forced the region to find alternative suppliers. Now, “it’s ships that matter more than pipes,” Hui states. “More than 80% of the oil and gas transiting the Strait of Hormuz is destined for Asia. For oil importers like Japan and South Korea, more than 60% of oil imports come from the Gulf. The abrupt nature of the disruption means these economies will rely on strategic reserves as a buffer.” Japan, South Korea and Taiwan typically hold at least 3–6 months of reserves, Hui says. However, a prolonged disruption could hit semiconductor and electronics production, and increase costs of building out data centres. “The region’s reliance on natural gas from the Middle East, and relatively low level of inventory given storage challenges, could be a more immediate concern, especially given the more sustained supply disruptions.” The market correction in both equities and fixed income securities could persists if the near term blockage at the Straight of Hormuz goes on. The impact on fixed income investors will depend on central banks. They are “more likely to hold rates steady and wait for conditions to stabilise than to rush into aggressive hikes. It is also worth remembering that global growth momentum was stable before the conflict began, supported by AI capex.” Economic activity supported by more diversified energy sourcing, such as China’s increasing renewables based supply, can act as a cushion. And should oil flows resume, investors can reconnect with positive momentum in areas such as data centre component demand and assembly, Hui adds. Long term, there may be a higher risk premium attached to energy from the GCC region if the blockade continues, alongside focus on renewables. However, Hui also argues that “eliminating risk exposure altogether by holding too much cash risk underperforming when the turnaround takes place.” Join our mailing list to receive our latest news updates, magazine features, thought leadership and market research & analysis. © 2026 Funds Europe Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Definite Article Media Limited. title: Malaysia, China stand out as Asia’s most resilient economies, says JP Morgan - Free Malaysia Today
content: The head of JP Morgan Asia, Rajiv Batra, told CNBC that beyond China and Malaysia, most economies in the region appear vulnerable to energy shocks. He noted that Malaysia’s strength lies in its energy export profile and disciplined policy framework, which provide a buffer against external pressures. “Malaysia’s fiscal deficit is very much well under control due to government policy, and inflation is not significantly high,” he said. These factors help support not only the country’s equity markets but also its currency amid external pressures, he added. For China, Rajiv noted that the country relies on imports for only 5% of its electricity generation, with the bulk of electricity generated domestically. In addition, China maintains substantial strategic reserves and retains the ability to scale up alternative energy sources, including coal and renewables, if needed. However, Rajiv warned that the broader outlook remains uncertain, stressing that global growth could come under pressure if geopolitical tensions continue to disrupt oil and gas supply chains – particularly through infrastructure damage and logistical delays. For equity markets, Rajiv said the immediate impact is expected in energy-sensitive sectors such as consumer goods, utilities, and downstream industries. However, he said, if the situation is prolonged, it could trigger a wider impact on financial, technology, telecommunications, and even healthcare. Despite these risks, Rajiv noted that markets have yet to fully price in a worst-case scenario and are still pricing a “muddle-through” scenario at this juncture. title: Is It Time To Reconsider JPMorgan Chase (JPM) After Recent Share Price Weakness? - simplywall.st
content:
JPMorgan Chase delivered 18.9% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Banks industry. The Excess Returns model looks at how effectively a bank turns shareholder equity into earnings above its required cost of capital. Instead of focusing on cash flows, it compares the return on equity to the cost of equity and capitalizes those “excess” profits into an implied value per share. For JPMorgan Chase, analysts point to a stable earnings power of about $24.23 per share on a stable book value of $144.34 per share. That is based on an average future return on equity of 16.79%. The implied cost of equity is $11.57 per share, which leaves an estimated excess return of $12.66 per share. Book value today is $126.99 per share, suggesting a solid equity base that the bank is expected to keep compounding according to these analyst estimates. When these excess returns are projected over time and discounted, the model arrives at an intrinsic value of about $419.14 per share. Compared with the recent share price around $282.84, the Excess Returns model indicates the stock is 32.5% undervalued within this framework. Our Excess Returns analysis suggests JPMorgan Chase is undervalued by 32.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 62 more high quality undervalued stocks. Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for JPMorgan Chase. P/E is a common yardstick for profitable companies because it links what you pay directly to the earnings the business is currently generating. For a bank like JPMorgan Chase, where earnings are a primary driver of shareholder returns, it gives you a straightforward way to compare price to profit. What counts as a “normal” or “fair” P/E depends on how quickly earnings are expected to grow and how risky those earnings appear. Higher growth or more resilient earnings can justify a higher P/E, while lower growth or higher risk usually points to a lower P/E range. JPMorgan Chase is trading on a P/E of 13.70x, compared with an industry average for Banks of 11.15x and a peer average of 12.17x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for JPMorgan Chase is 15.39x, which is a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given the company’s earnings profile, industry, margins, size and risk characteristics. This Fair Ratio can be more informative than simple peer or industry comparisons because it adjusts for those company specific factors. Since the Fair Ratio of 15.39x is above the current P/E of 13.70x, the shares appear undervalued on this metric. P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an easy tool used by millions of investors, where you pick or tailor a storyline, plug in assumptions, and see how that translates into a fair value that updates automatically when new news, earnings or guidance arrive. title: Morning Market Movements | Mixue Group Plummets Over 4% After J.P. Morgan Downgrades to Underweight - 富途牛牛
content: March 27,Mixue Group (02097)Yesterday's close was HK$300.6, today's open is HK$303.2, and as of press time the stock is trading at HK$286.0, down more than 4%. Following the release of its 2025 results, Mixue Group (02097.HK) has sparked concerns in the market about its future growth prospects and profitability. Although the company's revenue rose 35.2% year on year to RMB 33.56 billion last year, and net profit increased by 32.7% to RMB 5.88 billion,Gross margin declined from 32.5% to 31.1%., becoming the focus of market attention. The management also forecast that in 2026Strategically slowing the pace of store expansionFurthermore, the company plans to invest RMB 1.4 to 1.6 billion in supply-chain upgrades, which are expected to exert ongoing pressure on gross and net profit margins. In terms of operational data, the group's number of franchised store closures surged by 57% year on year to 2,527. At the same time, itsNet reduction of 428 overseas stores, primarily involving the adjustment and optimization of stores in the Indonesian and Vietnamese markets. In addition, the company announced a management restructuring,Founder Zhang Hongfu Steps Down as Chief Executive Officer (CEO), has been reassigned as Co-Chairman, with the position subsequently filled by former Chief Financial Officer Zhang Yuan, thereby introducing greater uncertainty into the company's strategic direction. From a technical perspective, both the MACD indicator and the 10-day/20-day moving averages have generated a "death cross" signal, suggesting that the stock price may face downward pressure. Short-selling data also warrants attention; as of March 25, itsBoth the number of shorted shares and the short interest ratio recorded significant increases., reflecting bearish market sentiment. Several major firms have adjusted their outlook on Mixue Group after the earnings release. Among them,JPMorgan has sharply downgraded its rating from "Overweight" to "Underweight."and willThe target price has been nearly halved from HK$521 to HK$270., indicating that the company's years of high growth may have come to an end. Other major banks also expressed cautious views: Goldman Sachs maintained a "buy" rating but lowered the target price to RMB 450; Daiwa also lowered the target price to RMB 350 while maintaining a "hold" rating; Bank of America Securities reiterated its "underperform" rating with a target price of RMB 353. Major banks are generally concerned about risks such as the slowdown in store expansion, pressure on gross profit margin, and rising costs. title: JP Morgan in line for business rates discount on Canary Wharf HQ - thetimes.com
content:
The government is lining up a tax break which could be worth hundreds of millions of pounds for America’s largest bank in order for it to build a new base at Canary Wharf. The Treasury is proposing a business rates discount of “up to 100 per cent” to attract JP Morgan Chase, one of the world’s largest financial institutions, to develop a European headquarters in Canary Wharf. That sits within the London borough of Tower Hamlets, one of the UK’s most deprived boroughs. The £3 billion project, which would be London’s largest office building, was first announced in November and was hailed by Rachel Reeves, the chancellor, as a “multi-billion pound vote of confidence in the UK economy”. It has emerged that JP Morgan has “requested a business rates incentive over a period of years” and the government has warned the local authority that the bank was “unlikely to progress” the project “without clarity and certainty” on its property tax bill, a council report said. The plan to provide a substantial tax incentive to one of the world’s biggest banking groups is highly contentious as it comes when many other businesses are facing a significant increase in their business rates bills. Kate Nicholls, chair of UK Hospitality, a trade group, said she had members in Tower Hamlets facing business rates increases of up to 200 per cent. She said: “This will be very hard for employers who are being asked to contribute more and more. “Hospitality pays nearly 75 per cent of profits to taxes while financial services institutions pay half that amount. To give tax breaks and incentives to companies who can afford to pay doesn’t feel fair or sustainable.” Sir Tim Martin, founder and chairman of JD Wetherspoon, which has pubs in the borough, also criticised the proposed subsidy. “The UK has gone from a competitively taxed to highly taxed economy in the last couple of decades,” he said. “This inevitably produces pork barrel politics whereby those who can negotiate discounts, while those who don’t have bargaining power are left to carry the can.” The development is due to be designed by Foster + Partners and is expected to accommodate up to 12,000 staff. At three million sq ft, the development, which could be completed by 2032, could have double the space of Britain’s tallest building, the Shard. It is claimed it could add £9.9 billion to the UK economy over six years and create about 7,800 construction-related jobs. Tower Hamlets has one of the highest rates of child poverty in the UK and the council says “huge wealth” in the borough exists alongside “deep poverty and inequality”. It had been expected that an agreement in principle on the subsidy proposals would be agreed by the end of March, but Tower Hamlets council has yet to receive details of the proposals in a memorandum of understanding (MoU) from the Treasury. A council report said a “risk” of the plan was the “significant long-term financial exposure” depending on the structure of the relief provided. The council said it was not willing to contribute to the cost of the subsidy. A spokeswoman for the council said: “The council has no intention of giving up any of its 30 per cent share of business rates. We are awaiting receipt of the high-level draft [of the MoU]. “We welcome JP Morgan Chase’s interest in Tower Hamlets, which would bring significant investment, thousands of jobs and development to our borough, which is already one of the most popular places to live, visit and work in the country, as well as being a huge coup for the UK economy, as the chancellor has made clear.” The council, which is being overseen by government officials as it deals with financial and governance issues, is expected to negotiate on details connected to the project including jobs and other social benefits. Those close to the discussions pointed out that construction projects on this scale often involve property tax incentives.The exact structure of any incentives has yet to be agreed. It is estimated that the site would generate tax of up to £1.6 billion over 25 years without any incentives. Locating the development in an “enterprise zone” could allow for a discount of “up to 100 per cent”, the council documents show. Enterprise zone rates discounts typically last five years. “JP Morgan’s board and stakeholders want certainty as they proceed with detailed design and contractor procurement,” the council’s report says. A spokesman for JP Morgan said the project “would represent a significant net benefit to both Tower Hamlets and the wider national economy as a whole”.The Greater London Authority, which is also being consulted, declined to comment. A Treasury spokesperson said: “We do not comment on tax speculation.” © Times Media Limited 2025.Registered in England No. 894646. Registered office: 1 London Bridge Street, SE1 9GF. title: Singapore Invites JPM, UBS to Collaborate on Creating Asian Gold Trading Hub - AASTOCKS.com
content: Singapore has invited major financial institutions, including JPMorgan (JPM.US)       and UBS, to participate in its vision to become an Asian gold trading hub, Nikkei Asia reported.The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) stated that these two global banks, together with DBS Bank, United Overseas Bank, the World Gold Council, and other institutions, have been included in a task force. This task force will formulate specific details and priorities for enhancing Singapore's gold ecosystem.According to the announcement, the task force will focus on developing gold-related capital market products to facilitate price discovery and establish liquidity, formulating storage and logistics standards that meet international norms, and establishing a clearing system to support secure over-the-counter settlement of large-scale gold bars and kilogram gold bars.(Real-time Streaming US Stocks Quote; Except All OTC quotes are at least 15 minutes delayed.)

title: The global rates repricing: Will central banks actually hike? - J.P. Morgan Private Bank
content:
The Private Bank’s mission is to build, preserve & transform our client’s wealth. We work with a variety of clients to help them achieve their unique ambitions. Our world-class economists, strategists, and investment specialists share their timely ideas and perspectives. We have worked with clients for more than 200 years to help them achieve their unique ambitions. Across the Atlantic, it’s a slightly different story. To illustrate just how large the repricing was, take the Bank of England. In a span of a few days, investors went from pricing in two rate cuts to over three rate hikes. In response to surging commodity prices, European and U.K. bond yields are trading near levels last seen in 2023, when global inflation was running near 10%.

 It was also when central banks were aggressively hiking rates in an attempt to bring down those prices. To avoid a sense of 2022 déjà vu, both the European Central Bank and the Bank of England are likely to respond given their single inflation-focused mandate.

 In a more severe scenario, where recession risks rise from a prolonged conflict, having exposure to duration could offer some portfolio protection. But it also benefits in a de-escalatory scenario, which is still the most likely outcome. 

 By extension, that means energy prices will ultimately trend lower, and commodities markets will eventually return to the oversupplied status that they began the year with. As the rates market in Europe digests the developments, yields are already providing some compensation. And as both an inevitable relief rally or a more extreme, but unlikely, recessionary scenario ensues, yields can be locked in at a higher level. title: JPM Keeps Overweight on CNOOC with TP Raised to HKD33, Higher Earnings Forecasts - AASTOCKS.com
content: According to a report from JPMorgan, CNOOC (00883.HK)  +0.580 (+1.996%)    Short selling $293.03M; Ratio 8.898%   's 4Q25 net profit reached RMB20 billion, which was below the broker's and the market's expectations of RMB26 billion and RMB29 billion, respectively. The underperformance was a result of higher-than-expected impairment charges and a slight increase in overall costs from new project launches.After factoring in oil price forecasts, JPMorgan has raised CNOOC's EPS forecasts for 2026-28 by 9-11% and increased its target price from HKD31 to HKD33.DataLast Update: 2026/03/30 09:16Integrated Oil & GasIndustry average cumulative performanceYTD +27.02%1-Month +5.43%Related News Citi: CNOOC (00883.HK) Last Year's Net Profit and Dividend Below Expectations, Maintains 'Buy' RatingGiven the ongoing Middle East conflict disrupting global oil and gas supplies, JPMorgan maintained an Overweight rating on CNOOC for its pure upstream business exposure. title: JPM Lowers Anta (02020.HK) TP to HKD130, Reiterates 'Overweight'; Market Share Expected to Further Increase - AASTOCKS.com
content: ANTA SPORTS (02020.HK)  -1.100 (-1.455%)    Short selling $120.29M; Ratio 11.607%   's 2025 results met expectations, with sales and core earnings growing by 13% and 14% YoY, respectively, according to a report from JPMorgan.The company guided that this year's core ANTA brand retail sales will see low single-digit growth, with an operating profit margin of about 20%, FILA retail sales will record mid-single-digit growth, with an operating profit margin of about 25%, and other brand retail sales will grow by more than 20%, with an operating profit margin of over 25%.DataLast Update: 2026/03/30 09:20ANTA SPORTSCumulative performance -7.51%YTD -12.40%1-MonthDataLast Update: 2026/03/30 09:16SportswearIndustry average cumulative performance -2.45%YTD -8.38%1-MonthRelated News UBS Lowers ANTA (02020.HK) TP to HKD112; Last Year's 2H Net Profit Exceeded ExpectationsWhile some investors consider ANTA SPORTS' guidance too conservative, JPMorgan believes it is realistic and highly visible, and the company's market share will further increase this year.JPMorgan lowered its target price for ANTA SPORTS from HKD141 to HKD130 but kept the Overweight rating unchanged.(HK stocks quote is delayed for at least 15 mins.Short Selling Data as at 2026-03-27 16:25.)

title: JPM Lowers KUAISHOU (01024.HK) TP to HKD48, Downgrades Rating to 'Neutral' - AASTOCKS.com
content: JPMorgan released a research report turning more cautious on KUAISHOU-W (01024.HK)  -1.080 (-2.344%)    Short selling $407.81M; Ratio 14.449%   's outlook over the next six months. The broker downgraded KUAISHOU-W from Overweight to Neutral, and slashed its target price from $89 to $48.JPMorgan expected core revenue growth, excluding the Kling AI business, to slow from 12% in 2025 to 3% in 2026/ 2027, mainly due to the impact of e-commerce regulation, weak consumption sentiment and the slowdown in growth areas such as short dramas due to competition from ByteDance.
